seo_description: Find the current local time in Karachi, Pakistan. Karachi uses Pakistan Standard Time (PKT), UTC+5 year-round with no daylight saving time. Live clock, time zone details, and FAQ.

faq.question: What time is it in Karachi right now?
faq.answer: Karachi, Pakistan uses Pakistan Standard Time (PKT, UTC+5) year-round. Pakistan does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Karachi. Karachi follows Pakistan Standard Time (PKT), which is UTC+5 year-round. For example, when it is 12:00 noon UTC, it is 5:00 PM in Karachi. Since Pakistan does not observe daylight saving time, the offset remains constant throughout the year, making it easy to calculate the current time in Karachi from any other time zone.

faq.question: What time zone is Karachi in?
faq.answer: Karachi is in the Pakistan Standard Time (PKT) zone, designated as Asia/Karachi in the IANA time zone database. PKT is UTC+5, meaning Karachi is 5 hours ahead of Coordinated Universal Time. This time zone applies uniformly across all of Pakistan, so every major city including Islamabad, Lahore, and Karachi shares the same local time.

faq.question: Does Karachi observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No, Karachi and the rest of Pakistan do not observe daylight saving time. Pakistan briefly experimented with DST in 2002 and again in 2008-2009 to address energy shortages, but the practice was discontinued after 2009 due to public opposition. As a result, Karachi remains at UTC+5 throughout the entire year with no clock changes.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Karachi and London?
faq.answer: Karachi is 5 hours ahead of London during the UK winter months when London is on Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/UTC+0). However, when the UK switches to British Summer Time (BST/UTC+1) from late March to late October, the difference narrows to 4 hours. For example, when it is 2:00 PM in Karachi, it is 9:00 AM in London during winter or 10:00 AM during summer.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Karachi and New York?
faq.answer: Karachi is 10 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5), which applies from November to mid-March. When New York switches to Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4) from mid-March to November, the gap reduces to 9 hours. So when it is 7:00 PM in Karachi, it is 9:00 AM in New York during EST or 10:00 AM during EDT.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Karachi and Dubai?
faq.answer: Karachi is just 1 hour ahead of Dubai. Dubai operates on Gulf Standard Time (GST/UTC+4), while Karachi is on PKT (UTC+5). Neither city observes daylight saving time, so this 1-hour difference remains constant year-round. This close time alignment reflects the strong business and travel ties between the two cities, with many direct flights connecting Karachi and Dubai daily.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Karachi and Delhi or Mumbai?
faq.answer: Karachi is 30 minutes behind Delhi and Mumbai. India follows Indian Standard Time (IST) at UTC+5:30, while Pakistan Standard Time (PKT) is UTC+5. Neither country currently observes daylight saving time, so this 30-minute difference is constant throughout the year. For example, when it is 12:00 noon in Karachi, it is 12:30 PM in Delhi and Mumbai.

faq.question: What are the typical business hours in Karachi?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Karachi are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM or 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday through Saturday. Many businesses and government offices observe a half-day on Friday, closing around 12:30 PM to 1:00 PM for Jumu'ah (Friday prayers), then reopening afterward or closing for the day. Banks typically operate from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Monday through Thursday and Saturday, with reduced Friday hours. The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) in Karachi trades from 9:30 AM to 3:30 PM PKT, Monday through Friday.
